LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER WISHING WELL FARMS' MOTION TO CONFIRM CONTRACTUAL ARBITRATION AWARD. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: WISHING WELL FARMS' PETITION TO CONFIRM CONTRACTUAL ARBITRATION AWARD AGAINST "RESPONDENTS FARMGIRL FLOWERS CHRISTINA MARIE STEMBEL" AND "NIEL RICHARD HILTZ" IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE ARBITRATION AWARDS DO NOT INDICATE THAT THE LATTER TWO PARTIES ARE RESPONDENTS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: E. LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER WISHING WELL FARMS' MOTION TO CONFIRM CONTRACTUAL ARBITRATION AWARD. NO APPEARANCE. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OFF CALENDAR. THE COURT DID NOT RECEIVE COURTESY COPIES OF THE FILINGS AS REQUIRED BY SF LOCAL RULE 2.7B. IN ADDITION, THE COURT'S REGISTER OF ACTIONS DOES NOT SHOW THAT PETITIONER SERVED BOTH THE UNDERLYING PETITION AND THE NOTICE OF MOTION. THE ONLY DOCUMENT THAT THE COURT CAN DETERMINE WAS SERVED IS THE REQUEST FOR COURT CALL APPEARANCE. JUDGE: ETHAN SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED

DECLARATION OF THOMAS V. CHRISTOPHER IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO CONFIRM CONTRACTUAL ARBITRATION AWARD BY RESPONDENT FARMGIRL FLOWERS, INC. (TRANSACTION ID # 63487279) FILED BY RESPONDENT FARMGIRL FLOWERS

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER WISHING WELL FARMS' MOTION TO CONFIRM CONTRACTUAL ARBITRATION AWARD IS GRANTED. (CODE CIV. PROC. 1286.) RESPONDENT FARMGIRL FLOWERS DOES NOT CONTEND THAT THERE ARE GROUNDS FOR VACATING THE AWARD. (CODE CIV. PROC. 1286.2.) PETITIONER'S REQUEST TO INTRODUCE ORAL EVIDENCE AT THE HEARING IS DENIED. (CAL. R. CT. 3.1306(A).) THE COURT WILL ENTER JUDGMENT ON THE AWARD IN THE AMOUNT OF $146,572.20. COUNSEL FOR THE PETITIONER IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND A SEPARATE PROPOSED JUDGMENT. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR#6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM, NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Jul-18-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER WISHING WELL FARMS' Motion To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award.

Petitioner Wishing Well Farms' motion to confirm contractual arbitration award is granted. (Code Civ. Proc. 1286.) Respondent Farmgirl Flowers does not contend that there are grounds for vacating the award. (Code Civ. Proc. 1286.2.) Petitioner's request to introduce oral evidence at the hearing is denied. (Cal. R. Ct. 3.1306(a).) The Court will enter judgment on the award in the amount of $146,572.20.

Counsel for the petitioner is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and a separate proposed judgment.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award
Off Calendar May-28-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER WISHING WELL FARMS' MOTION TO CONFIRM CONTRACTUAL ARBITRATION AWARD

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Wishing Well Farms' petition to confirm contractual arbitration award against "respondents Farmgirl Flowers Christina Marie Stembel" and "Niel Richard Hiltz" is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. The arbitration awards do not indicate that the latter two parties are respondents. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600 (Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com) (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Apr-22-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER WISHING WELL FARMS' Motion To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award

Off calendar. The court did not receive courtesy copies of the filings as required by SF Local Rule 2.7B. In addition, the court's register of actions does not show that petitioner served both the underlying petition and the notice of motion. The only document that the court can determine was served is the request for court call appearance.

Judge: Ethan Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)
